When working with Chibi bot

1. When editing:
    - Ensure your code adheres to the project's coding style and conventions.
    - Write clear and concise comments to explain complex logic.
    - Errors are handled gracefully with informative messages.
    - Follow Typescripts's best practices for performance and safety.
    - Make sure texts are UTF-8 Encoded only.

2. When adding features:
    - Ensure new features integrate seamlessly with existing functionality.
    - Write modular and reusable code where possible.
    - Validate all inputs to prevent errors and security vulnerabilities.
    
3. When fixing bugs:
    - Identify the root cause of the issue before applying a fix.
    - Test the fix in various scenarios to ensure it resolves the issue without introducing new problems.

4. When finalizing changes:
    - Test thoroughly to ensure new features work as intended and do not introduce bugs.
    - Review code for readability and maintainability.
    - Do not create documentations or test files unless explicitly instructed.
    - Remove any temp tests files if they were made.
    - Only create summaries on the chat.


P.S: DO NOT CREATE DOCUMENTATIONS OR SUMMARY FILES.
